excerpt: Complete breakdown of Elite Trader Funding pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Elite Trader Funding Pricing Overview

Elite Trader Funding offers a straightforward pricing model designed for futures traders seeking rapid market access. Starting at $75 per month, the platform provides multiple evaluation pathways with a clear cost structure. Unlike many prop firms that bundle features and lock traders into single evaluation types, Elite Trader Funding distinguishes itself by offering six different evaluation options, giving traders flexibility to choose the path that matches their trading style and capital goals. The pricing ranges from the entry-level 1-Step Evaluation at $75/month to the premium Direct to Funded plan at $599/month—making it accessible to casual futures traders while catering to experienced professionals targeting immediate funding.

What sets Elite Trader Funding apart is not just the entry cost but the profit structure. Once funded, traders receive a 100% profit split on the first $12,500 in lifetime sim-funded profits, meaning every dollar earned during early stages returns entirely to the trader. Combined with aggressive promotional discounts (often 50–90% off evaluation fees), the true cost to get started is frequently much lower than the standard pricing suggests.

All Elite Trader Funding Plans Compared

Elite Trader Funding offers four primary evaluation tiers, each targeting different trader archetypes and funding timelines:

Plan Name Monthly Cost Best For Key Features
1-Step Evaluation $75/month Traders wanting affordable, straightforward evaluation with no rush to fund Standard evaluation path, paper trading, mobile app access, performance analytics
Fast Track $75/month Traders capable of passing evaluation quickly; same-day funding possible Accelerated evaluation path, potential same-day live account setup, paper trading, broker integration
Diamond Hands $365/month Experienced traders wanting news trading and more relaxed drawdown rules Premium features, news trading allowed, looser risk parameters, performance analytics
Direct to Funded $599/month Professional traders seeking immediate live account without evaluation Instant funding (no evaluation period), maximum account sizes, news trading, minimum trading days waived on select accounts

1-Step Evaluation ($75/month): This is Elite Trader Funding's most accessible entry point. Traders pay $75 monthly to begin an evaluation period on a simulated trading account. There's no rush—evaluations don't expire quickly, giving traders time to demonstrate consistent profitability. Once approved, the trader moves to a live funded account and begins earning the 100% profit split on their first $12,500. The evaluation includes full access to the mobile app, paper trading infrastructure, performance analytics, and risk management tools.

Fast Track ($75/month): Despite the identical $75 monthly price, Fast Track accelerates the path to funding. Traders who pass evaluation quickly may access a live trading account on the same day, eliminating delays for those confident in their abilities. This tier appeals to experienced scalpers and intraday futures traders who want rapid deployment. The Fast Track includes all base features plus expedited account activation and broker integration for seamless order routing.

Diamond Hands ($365/month): Jumping to $365 per month, Diamond Hands targets experienced traders seeking premium conditions. The primary distinction is the allowance for news trading and relaxed risk parameters—key for traders employing momentum and event-driven strategies. News trading is typically restricted in prop firms due to volatility, so this feature appeals to traders with specific expertise. The higher monthly cost reflects both premium features and potentially larger account sizes available at this tier. The evaluation period is shorter, and account limits are higher.

Direct to Funded ($599/month): Elite Trader Funding's premium tier skips evaluation entirely. For $599 per month, traders receive an instantly funded live account without passing any evaluation milestones. This tier targets professionals with significant trading histories and capital reserves who want immediate market access. Direct to Funded accounts come with the highest account sizes (up to $300,000), news trading permissions, and minimum trading day requirements waived on select plans. It's the fastest path to profitability for proven traders.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Elite Trader Funding does not offer a free tier or free trial. All evaluation pathways require the upfront monthly fee. However, this doesn't mean there's no low-cost entry point. The platform frequently runs promotional campaigns offering 50–90% discounts on evaluation fees, effectively reducing the $75 entry cost to $7.50–$37.50 for the first month. During these promotions, new traders can begin evaluation at a fraction of standard pricing.

Traders without access to promotional codes can still minimize risk by starting with the 1-Step Evaluation at $75, which extends the evaluation timeline indefinitely. Unlike time-limited trials, this structure lets traders take weeks or months to demonstrate profitability without time pressure. The absence of a free tier is typical in the prop trading industry—the monthly fee ensures commitment and filters for serious traders, reducing account abandonment and support costs.

Hidden Costs and Fees

While Elite Trader Funding's published pricing is relatively transparent, several costs exist beyond the evaluation fees:

  • $80/month Activation Fee: Once a trader passes evaluation and receives funding, an $80 monthly fee applies to the live funded account. This is a significant ongoing cost separate from the evaluation fee. A trader on the 1-Step Evaluation ($75/month) plus the activation fee ($80/month) faces a total monthly commitment of $155 once funded.
  • Futures Exchange Fees: Futures trading incurs exchange fees (CME, ICE, etc.) and clearing costs. These aren't unique to Elite Trader Funding but are mandatory when trading live contracts. Traders should budget $20–$50+ per month depending on trading volume.
  • Data Feed Costs: Some traders may need premium market data feeds (Level 2, advanced charting). These typically run $50–$200 monthly depending on the provider and are not included in the base pricing.
  • Account Restrictions: The maximum of 5 active sim-funded accounts (reduced from 20 in September 2025) means traders managing multiple strategies may need to choose which accounts to fund, potentially delaying profitability on secondary strategies.

The $80/month activation fee is worth noting in total cost calculations. Over a year, it represents $960 in funding-related fees beyond the evaluation cost, effectively doubling or tripling the cost of entry for cost-conscious traders.

Elite Trader Funding Pricing vs Competitors

Elite Trader Funding's pricing is competitive within the prop trading sector, though the feature set varies by competitor:

Prop Firm Starting Price Activation Fee Markets Funding Speed
Elite Trader Funding $75/month (with 50–90% promo discounts available) $80/month (once funded) Futures only Same-day funding possible via Fast Track
TopStep (approximate) $99–$149/month $150–$300 (one-time, varies by tier) Equities, some futures 5–10 business days typical
The5ers (approximate) $89–$299/month Included in tier pricing Forex, equities, some cryptos 3–5 business days
Funded Trader Plus (approximate) $59–$299/month Varies by tier Forex, equities, indices Same-day to 3 days

Elite Trader Funding's $75 base price is competitive, often matching or undercutting alternatives. The key differentiator is the futures-only focus—traders in forex, equities, or options will need to look elsewhere. However, for dedicated futures traders, the combination of affordable entry, same-day funding options, and aggressive discounts makes Elite Trader Funding one of the more cost-effective choices. The ongoing $80/month activation fee is higher than some competitors' one-time fees but reflects the recurring cost of maintaining a live funded account.

Is Elite Trader Funding Worth the Price?

For Scalpers & High-Frequency Futures Traders: Yes. The $75–$599 range is justified if you trade futures actively. The 100% profit split on the first $12,500 means early earnings return entirely to you, offsetting the evaluation and activation fees quickly. Same-day funding options accelerate capital deployment for traders ready to execute.

For Swing Traders (Futures): Moderately yes. The value depends on your profitability speed. If you can pass evaluation within 2–3 months, the total cost ($75×3 months + $80 activation = $305) is reasonable. If evaluation takes 6+ months, costs accumulate and other platforms might be cheaper.

For News Traders & Event-Driven Strategies: Yes, especially Diamond Hands ($365/month) and Direct to Funded ($599/month). The allowance for news trading is rare in prop firms and justifies the premium for traders with proven expertise in this style.

For Forex, Options, or Equity Traders: No. Elite Trader Funding is futures-exclusive. Alternative platforms like TopStep or The5ers offer better value if you trade other instruments.

How to Save on Elite Trader Funding

  • Promotional Discounts (50–90% off): Elite Trader Funding runs frequent promotional campaigns reducing the $75–$599 monthly cost by 50–90%. Sign up during promotions to slash first-month costs. Check their website, trading communities, and affiliate partners for active codes.
  • Pass Evaluation Quickly: The faster you move from evaluation to funding, the lower your total evaluation costs. A trader passing in 2 weeks at $75 spends only $150 on evaluation fees, while one taking 3 months spends $225.
  • Combine 1-Step + Fast Track Strategy: Both are priced at $75/month. Start with 1-Step to learn the platform, then upgrade to Fast Track for same-day funding once confident. This spreads cost over time rather than committing to premium tiers upfront.
  • Negotiate Activation Fees: For Direct to Funded traders, the $80/month activation is standard, but large-volume traders may negotiate with customer success teams. This is less common but worth exploring if you're committing to six-figure accounts.
  • Monitor Account Limits: With a maximum of 5 active sim-funded accounts, consolidate your capital into fewer, larger accounts rather than maintaining multiple small ones. This reduces duplicate activation fees and simplifies risk management.

The most significant savings opportunity is catching promotional periods—a 75% discount reduces the $75 entry to just $18.75, making the first-month barrier nearly negligible.
